data.title: US unsecured loan balances hit record high on demand from subprime customers
data.description:
Key points:
  • Subprime clients using loans to consolidate debt, manage living costs
  • Credit card balances rise 4% as issuers focus on riskier clients
  • TransUnion forecasts slower growth in new credit for 2026

By Tatiana Bautzer

Robust demand from subprime customers spurred growth in U.S. unsecured loans last year with their combined balances surging 10% to a new high of $276 billion, according to TransUnion's Credit Industry Insights Report.

Some 26.4 million consumers carried those loans as of end-December, up from 24.5 million a year earlier.

"As interest rates began to fall, many consumers are consolidating their credit card balances into unsecured loans," said Michele Raneri, vice president and head of U.S. research and consulting at TransUnion.

Lower-income consumers are also using these loans as a stopgap measure to deal with higher costs of living that have not been followed by similar raises in wages, she added.

Credit card issuers have increased lending to lower-income consumers, with total balances rising 4% last year to $1.15 trillion. But they have reduced initial credit limits to deal with the risk, the report said. Delinquency rates have been slowly rising over recent quarters.

SLOWER GROWTH

TransUnion forecasts slower growth this year for the volume of new credit extended.

Raneri said the credit markets are now going back to more 'normal' growth levels, after strong fluctuations since the pandemic.

On Thursday, the credit bureau revised its previous forecast of a 5.7% rise in new unsecured loans in 2026 to 11.2%. TransUnion projects a 4% rise in mortgages and 4.2% climb in home refinancings.

"People that have recent mortgages taken with higher interest rates are starting to have access to refinancing and we expect that demand to grow," TransUnion's vice president added.

Auto loans are expected to shrink 1.5% this year, after having risen around 5% last year with consumers accelerating purchases to avoid the impact of import tariffs.

data.title: Mandelson's consultancy firm set to enter administration after Epstein links
data.description:
Global Counsel, the consultancy firm co-founded by former British ambassador to the United States Peter Mandelson, is preparing to enter administration as early as Friday, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters.

Staff were told on Thursday that the move followed a wave of client departures after disclosures about Mandelson's past links to the late U.S. sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, the source said.

The firm did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Global Counsel cut ties with Mandelson in September last year, Reuters had reported, the day Prime Minister Keir Starmer sacked him as Britain's ambassador to Washington.

Benjamin Wegg-Prosser, who co-founded Global Counsel alongside Mandelson, stepped down as chief executive earlier in February.

data.title: ICE Canola Down Despite Oil Strength
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange declined on Thursday morning, despite rising crude and vegetable oil prices.

Chicago soyoil, European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were up. Crude oil was on the rise due to concerns over an increasing United States military presence in the Middle East. Also, peace talks between Russia and Ukraine ended abruptly on Wednesday.

The Canadian dollar was down more than one-tenth of a U.S. cent compared to Wednesday's close.

Nearly 18,500 contracts were traded. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton as of 8:33 CST:

 
           Price      Change 
Mar       667.80     dn 2.40 
May       680.10     dn 2.60 
Jul       690.60     dn 2.90 
Nov       685.00     dn 2.10

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

London, 19 February 2026

Lion Finance Group PLC announces cancellation of shares and completion of buyback programme

Lion Finance Group PLC (the "Company"- LSE: BGEO LN) announces that the Company has cancelled 38,580 treasury shares bought back under the GEL 51.5 million share buyback and cancellation programme which was announced on 20 November 2025 as an extension of the GEL 98.0 million share buyback and cancellation programme announced on 20 August 2025 (the "Buyback Programme").

The Company has completed the GEL 51.5 million share buyback. The total number of shares cancelled since the launch of the Buyback Programme in August 2025 is 497,669. All the treasury shares purchased in the Buyback Programme have now been cancelled.

data.title: U.S. Jobless Claims Decrease Much More Than Expected To 206,000
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - First-time claims for U.S. unemployment benefits fell by much more than expected in the week ended February 14th, according to a report released by the Labor Department on Thursday.

The report said initial jobless claims slid to 206,000, a decrease of 23,000 from the previous week's revised level of 229,000.

Economists had expected jobless claims to slip to 225,000 from the 227,000 originally reported for the previous week.

'The renewed decline in initial jobless claims back toward 200,000 is consistent with other signs that labor market conditions are stabilizing,' said Michael Pearce, Chief U.S. Economist at Oxford Economics. 'The labor market remains in a low-hire, low-fire equilibrium.'

The Labor Department said the less volatile four-week moving average also edged down to 219,000, a decrease of 1,000 from the previous week's revised average of 220,000.

Meanwhile, the report said continuing claims, a reading on the number of people receiving ongoing unemployment assistance, rose by 17,000 to 1.869 million in the week ended February 7th.

The four-week moving average of continuing claims also inched up to 1,845,250, an increase of 1,000 from the previous week's revised average of 1,844,250.

'Continued claims ticked higher in the week ended February 7, but the recent pattern of downward revisions persists,' said Pearce. 'Hiring remains subdued, but the stability in continued claims suggests conditions are not deteriorating.'

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Deere Stock Is on Track for a Record High — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Shares in Deere jumped in Thursday morning trade after the company said demand is improving for its small farm tractors and construction and forestry equipment.

  • The stock rose more than 7% to top $638.
  • Shares were on pace to finish at an all-time high if they hold on to gains through the session. As of yesterday's close, the stock was up 27% year to date.
  • The company boosted its outlook for the year, even though quarterly earnings were hit by higher tariffs and other costs.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: TSX Gives Up Record Peak
data.description:
The S&P/TSX Composite edged around 0.3% lower to below the 33,300 mark, pulling back from record levels as hawkish undertones from the latest Federal Reserve minutes pressured rate sensitive sectors and prompted profit taking in technology and gold miners.

Financial heavyweights including Royal Bank of Canada and Bank of Montreal traded modestly lower, while bullion eased from recent highs, weighing on Agnico Eagle (-0.8%), Barrick Gold (-0.7%), and Wheaton Precious Metals (-0.6%).

Losses in materials were compounded by a sharp drop in Teck Resources (-4%) despite its earnings beat, as investors locked in gains following a strong run.

Energy shares provided partial support as crude held firm on Middle East supply risks, lifting Canadian Natural Resources, Imperial Oil and Cenovus Energy.

data.title: Pakistan, US sign pact to redevelop New York's Roosevelt Hotel
data.description:
Key points:
  • Deal allows Islamabad to unlock value of one of its most prized overseas investments
  • Agreement comes as Pakistan deepens economic engagement with Washington

By Ariba Shahid

Pakistan has signed a deal with the U.S. government to jointly redevelop New York's Roosevelt Hotel, according to documents seen by Reuters and two sources, allowing Islamabad to unlock the value of one of its most prized overseas investments.

The century-old hotel, owned by Pakistan's national airline, has been closed since 2020 and is central to Islamabad’s IMF-backed asset restructuring, which the government has previously estimated could be worth more than $1 billion.

The two countries signed the memorandum on Thursday after Pakistan's cabinet approved the process, two sources with knowledge of the matter told Reuters. They were not authorised to speak publicly on the matter.

DEEPENING ECONOMIC TIES

Reuters saw a document showing a cabinet summary saying the prime minister cleared the proposal by authorising the Ministry of Defence to sign the non-binding memorandum with the U.S. General Services Administration (GSA), as part of efforts to enhance bilateral and commercial relations.

Pakistan's defense ministry and privatisation ministry did not respond to a request for comment. The White House and U.S. Embassy in Islamabad also did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The agreement comes as Pakistan deepens economic engagement with Washington, including U.S. financing support for the Reko Diq copper and gold mining project in Pakistan's Balochistan, partly owned by Barrick Resources.

Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif is currently in Washington to attend the inaugural Board of Peace meeting.

The memorandum, seen by Reuters, says the project "shall be facilitated by the United States General Services Administration and by the Pakistan Ministry of Defence", and will cover the "renovation, operation, maintenance, and redevelopment" of the property near Grand Central Terminal.

It does not specify financial terms.

The U.S. General Services Administration primarily manages federal property and procurement for U.S. government agencies, and its publicly stated mandate does not typically include commercial redevelopment of foreign state-owned assets. It was not immediately clear under what authority the agency would facilitate the project.

data.title: CNA: 2025 saw resilient performance amid earnings decline, major infrastructure investment, and enhanced shareholder returns
data.description:
Earnings declined in 2025 due to market and operational headwinds, but strategic investments in nuclear and LNG infrastructure, improved customer metrics, and disciplined capital allocation position the business for long-term growth. Shareholder returns increased, and digital transformation continues.

data.title: Walmart Earnings Beat Estimates. Why the Market Is Shrugging. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Sabrina Escobar

Walmart narrowly beat fourth-quarter earnings estimates but full-year guidance was softer than expected as the company seeks to set a lower bar for its first year under new CEO John Furner.

Walmart's revenue rose 5.6% year over year to $190.7 billion, slightly better than consensus estimates calling for $190.5 billion, according to FactSet.

Adjusted earnings per share of 74 cents were modestly above projections for 73 cents.

The company's guidance fell short of expectations, however. Walmart predicts that sales for the fiscal year ending next January will increase between 3.5% and 4.5%, and that adjusted earnings per share will range from $2.75 to $2.85.

Wall Street had projected sales would grow by about 5% on an annual basis, with earnings per share of $2.97.

Walmart's first-quarter adjusted earnings per share outlook also was softer than expected. The company sees earnings ranging from 63 cents to 65 cents a share. The higher end of the range is below analysts' projections for 68 cents.

Shares of Walmart fell 2.9% in premarket trading Thursday.

The guidance miss wasn't altogether unexpected. Walmart has a history of issuing conservative outlooks, and its current CEO transition likely made the company even more cautious. Furner also noted that while spending remains resilient in the U.S., consumers are choiceful. Much of the company's market share gains continue to come form households making more than $100,000, he added. Spending is tighter on the lower end of the income spectrum.

"For household earnings below $50,000, we continue to see that wallets are stretched, and in some cases people are managing spending paycheck to paycheck," Furner said.

That said, that the stock is trading lower also is unsurprising. The stock came into the report trading at "extreme levels" and a significant guidance beat is one of the few things that could have moved shares higher after the report, said Jay Woods, chief market strategist at Freedom Capital Markets.

Earlier this year, Walmart became the first traditional retailer to command a trillion-dollar market capitalization. The stock has gained about 14% this year as of Wednesday's close and trades a price to earnings ratio of 43 times the next 12 months' earnings — higher than all Magnificent Seven stocks outside of Tesla.

The company may be hoping that a higher dividend and a new share repurchase program may soften the guidance blow. Walmart's board authorized $30 billion in share repurchases and raised its annual dividend for the 53rd consecutive year. The new dividend of 99 cents a share is 5% higher than the 94 cents paid in the last fiscal year. This is the 53rd consecutive year of dividend increases.

Indeed, bulls were unshaken by the soft guidance. Jefferies analyst Corey Tarlowe noted that he expects Walmart is well positioned heading into the new fiscal year, with a balanced growth algorithm and improving earnings. Any near-term dips in the stock price present an opportunity for investors, he added.

Michael Baker, an analyst at D.A. Davidson, agreed.

"Outside of the guidance, most metrics for 4Q were solid and continue to show WMT as a share gainer, offering value for low income customers and convenience for higher earning households, powered by perhaps the best tech strategy in retail," he wrote Thursday.

This is the first report under Furner, who recently became the retailer's sixth CEO in about as many decades.

Furner's debut is a big deal. He is taking the reins of the world's largest retailer at a critical crossroads for the industry. The way Walmart adapts to a new era of shopping powered by artificial intelligence could set the standard for other retailers, making Furner's vision for the company all the more important for investors and competitors alike.

Furner's predecessor, Doug McMillon, laid the groundwork for the company's successful digital transformation, which culminated in Walmart's stock switching over to the Nasdaq Stock Market from the New York Stock Exchange last year. Fourth-quarter results reflect the change — Walmart's e-commerce sales grew 24% from a year ago, and revenue from the company's fledgling advertising business rose 37%.

While Furner has officially been in the role for less than a month, markets are already itching to learn more about his priorities and how he plans to keep the company's momentum and market share gains going.

Write to Sabrina Escobar at sabrina.escobar@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Tesla Stock Falls. Why Retail Investors Keep Buying Alongside Micron, Microsoft. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Tesla stock remains popular with retail investors despite recent declines.

Shares of the electric vehicle maker were down 0.7% at $408.50 in early trading on Thursday, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were both down about 0.3%.

Early losses leave Tesla stock down about 5% since the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter earnings on Jan. 28.

Some investors appear to be worried about valuation; Tesla stock trades at about 210 times estimated 2026 earnings, or they are waiting for a new catalyst. Tesla plans to expand its robot-taxi service into nine cities in the first half of 2026. It currently operates commercially in one city, Austin, Texas.

One group adding to their Tesla positions, however, is retail investors.

Investors have been finding refuge in non-AI stocks lately, wrote JPMorgan strategist Arun Jain in a Thursday research note. Coming into Thursday trading, the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF was down more than 6% so far this year, underperforming the S&P 500 by about seven percentage points. Still, "retail investors remained firm in their stock allocation," adding to Nvidia, Amazon.com, Microsoft, Tesla, Sandisk, and Micron Technology positions this past week.

Tesla was the fourth-most-purchased stock after Nvidia, Amazon, and Microsoft.

Whether retail buying is a harbinger of better returns in the coming weeks for Tesla shares is hard to say. Coming into Thursday trading, Tesla stock was down about 9% so far this year.

Even if the stock doesn't turn around, it's worth checking in on retail trading activity. Tesla is a very popular stock among smaller investors. Retail investors hold about 40% of Tesla stock, according to Bloomberg, about twice the level of other large tech stocks.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: US Stocks Pull Back on Thursday
data.description:
US equity indices were firmly lower on Thursday, trimming the gains yesterday on signs that the Federal Reserve may maintain its rates elevated for longer.

The three main averages fell up to 0.5%.

Rates on the longer end of the curve rebounded as hawkish remarks on the FOMC minutes were combined with the sharp increase in crude oil prices and softer initial claim counts halfway through February.

The minutes indicated that a majority of policymakers were concerned that disinflation may take longer than usual, while few noted that higher rates may be necessary if inflation stabilizes above target.

Banks and lenders were lower on the jump in rates, while tech giants inched down on risks to the elevated levels of capital expenditure pledges for datacenters.

Nvidia and Metal dropped 0.5%, while JP Morgan and Bank of America fell over 1%.

Meanwhile, Walmart jumped 2% after beating Q4 revenue estimates and raising its dividend, while Deere added 7% after its earnings.

data.title: Goosehead Insurance, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Goosehead Insurance, Inc., a leading provider of personal lines insurance, has released its 2025 10-K report, showcasing significant financial and operational achievements. The report highlights the company's robust revenue growth, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $365.3 million, increased by 16% from 2024 driven by growth in both corporate and franchise sales.

Operating Income: $74.4 million, reflecting an increase due to higher revenues and improved cash collections.

Net Income: $44.5 million, decreased by $4.7 million from 2024, impacted by increased interest expense and tax expense.

Basic EPS: $1.11, reflecting a decrease from the previous year due to higher operating expenses and interest costs.

Diluted EPS: $1.04, decreased from $1.16 in 2024, influenced by increased operating expenses and interest expense.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates primarily in personal lines insurance, with significant contributions from homeowner's insurance (63% of Total Written Premium), automotive insurance (34%), and commercial insurance (2%).

Sales Units: Policies in Force increased by 14% from 1.67 million in 2024 to 1.9 million in 2025, indicating strong growth in the number of insurance policies managed.

Geographical Performance: The company has a strong presence across the United States, with franchise locations covering over 97% of the U.S. population. Texas, Florida, and California are among the states with the highest number of operating agencies.

New Product Launches: The company continues to enhance its Digital Agent platform, which allows clients to shop for home and auto insurance rates with over 100 top carriers, providing a streamlined and efficient customer experience.

Operational Efficiency: The company has centralized service centers that handle client service and renewals, contributing to a high client retention rate of 85% and a premium retention rate of 90% in 2025.

Agent Productivity: Corporate sales agents with more than three years of tenure averaged 2.5 times as much New Business Production per Agent as the industry best practice, while franchise sales agents averaged 2.0 times.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue expanding its recruiting efforts for corporate sales agents and franchisees, aiming to increase market share and productivity through innovative technology and enhanced service capabilities.

Strategic Initiatives

Growth Strategy: The company has focused on expanding its national footprint and increasing its revenue-producing headcount as part of its growth strategy. It has also invested significantly in its proprietary technology platform to drive scalability and efficiency, which is considered a key competitive advantage. Additionally, the company is actively marketing for new franchises in established markets, which cover over 97% of the U.S. population.

Capital Management: The company issued a $300 million term loan to repay a previous term loan and fund a special distribution and dividend of $205 million. It also repurchased $81.7 million of its Class A common stock under a share repurchase program. The board of directors approved a new share repurchase program with authorization to purchase up to $100 million of Class A common stock through May 1, 2026, which was later extended and increased by $180 million through May 1, 2027. The company declared dividends totaling $145.8 million, equating to $5.91 per share.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its focus on organic growth by investing in people and technology. It expects to fund its working capital requirements and commitments through cash on hand, net working capital, cash flows from operations, and its Revolving Credit Facility. The company aims to maintain its growth trajectory by retaining top talent and expanding its franchise network, while also managing its debt obligations and exploring further opportunities for share repurchases and dividends.

Challenges and Risks

Economic and Market Risks: An overall decline in economic activity, characterized by international trade uncertainty, inflationary pressures, and geopolitical tensions, could materially affect the company's financial condition. Changes in interest rates and U.S. monetary policies could also impact the demand for insurance products, affecting revenue generation.

Insurance Industry Risks: Volatility or declines in insurance premiums, along with trends towards alternative insurance markets, could undermine profitability. The company's revenue is heavily dependent on commission rates set by Carriers, which are subject to change.

Operational Risks: The company is highly reliant on information processing systems, making it vulnerable to security breaches and cyberattacks. Additionally, the inability to recover from disasters or business continuity problems could cause significant financial and reputational damage.

Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to extensive regulation, and non-compliance or changes in laws could restrict business operations. The insurance industry is under scrutiny, and new regulations could impact compensation models and operational practices.

Franchise Business Risks: The success of the franchise model depends on attracting and retaining qualified Franchisees. Conflicts with Franchisees or failure to support the expanding franchise system could adversely affect the company's financial condition.

Intellectual Property and Cybersecurity Risks: The company faces risks related to the infringement of intellectual property rights and the improper disclosure of confidential information, which could lead to legal liability and reputational harm.

Climate and Environmental Risks: Climate change poses physical risks and could lead to increased claims and reduced Carrier capacity. Regulatory changes related to climate could also increase operational costs.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by economic volatility and competitive pressures. Strategies to address these include enhancing technological capabilities and expanding the franchise network to mitigate risks associated with market fluctuations and operational disruptions.

Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations and changes in Carrier relationships, which could impact revenue streams. Management is focused on maintaining strong relationships with Carriers and exploring new market opportunities to diversify revenue sources.

SEC Filing: Goosehead Insurance, Inc. [ GSHD ] - 10-K - Feb. 19, 2026
